cards.back: A one-way mathematical function that takes input data of any size and produces a fixed-size string of characters, known as a hash or digest
A mechanism by which nodes on a network agree on the validity of a block of transactions, ensuring the integrity of the blockchain
A unique identifier used by a user to access their digital wallet and manage their cryptocurrency
The process by which new blocks are added to the blockchain, requiring computational power and incentivized by rewards, such as newly minted coins
A self-executing program that automates the enforcement and execution of an agreement or contract on a blockchain
